title: Data API builder
description: Schema for Data API builder engine

$schema.description: URI of the JSON schema with which the structure, constraints, and data types of this Data API builder runtime configuration file should conform.

connection-string.description: Connection string for the selected database type

options.description: Database specific properties for the backend database

enabled.description: Enable health check endpoint for something

name.description: Identifier for data source in health check report.

threshold-ms.description: Health Check maximum time for query to execute (in ms).

user-delegated-auth.description: User-delegated authentication configuration for On-Behalf-Of (OBO) flow. Enables DAB to connect to the database using the calling user's identity.

database-audience.description: The audience URI for the target database (e.g., https://database.windows.net for Azure SQL).

options.description: Database specific properties for the backend database

schema.description: Path to the GraphQL schema file

set-session-context.description: Enable sending data to MsSql using session context

data-source-files.description: Names of runtime configuration files referencing additional databases.

pagination.description: Pagination settings for REST and GraphQL API responses.

max-page-size.description: Defines the maximum number of records that can be returned in a single page of results. If set to null, the default value is 100,000.

default-page-size.description: Sets the default number of records returned in a single response. When this limit is reached, a continuation token is provided to retrieve the next page. If set to null, the default value is 100.

next-link-relative.description: When true, nextLink in paginated results will use a relative URL.

path.description: URL prefix path for all REST entity endpoints.

enabled.description: Allow enabling/disabling REST requests for all entities.

request-body-strict.description: Does not allow extraneous fields in request body when set to true.

allow-introspection.description: Allow querying of the underlying GraphQL schema.

path.description: URL prefix path for the GraphQL endpoint.

enabled.description: Allow enabling/disabling GraphQL requests for all entities.

depth-limit.description: Maximum allowed depth of a GraphQL query.

multiple-mutations.description: Configuration properties for multiple mutation operations

enabled.description: Allow enabling/disabling multiple create operations for all entities.

path.description: URL prefix path for MCP endpoints.

enabled.description: Allow enabling/disabling MCP requests for all entities.

description.description: Description of the MCP server, exposed as the 'instructions' field in the MCP initialize response to provide behavioral context to MCP clients and agents.

dml-tools.description: Configuration for MCP Data Manipulation Language (DML) tools. Set to true/false to enable/disable all tools, or use an object to configure individual tools.

oneOf.description: Enable/disable all DML tools with default settings.

aggregate-records.description: Enable/disable or configure the aggregate-records MCP tool.

query-timeout.description: Execution timeout in seconds for aggregate queries. Range: 1-600.

max-response-size-mb.description: Specifies the maximum size, in megabytes, of the database response allowed in a single result. If set to null, the default value is 158 MB.

mode.description: Set if running in Development or Production mode

authentication.description: Authentication settings for the runtime host.

oneOf.description: Authentication provided by Azure Static Web Apps.
Authentication provided by Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD). Use the JWT property to configure this provider.
Simulated authentication for development and testing purposes.
Authentication provided by Azure App Service.
Synonymous with the EntraID value. Use the JWT property to configure this provider.
Custom authentication provider defined by the user. Use the JWT property to configure the custom provider.
Unauthenticated provider where all operations run as anonymous. Use when Data API builder is behind an app gateway or APIM where authentication is handled externally.

jwt.description: JWT token validation settings. Required when using a JWT-based identity provider (e.g., EntraID, AzureAD, or Custom).

audience.description: The expected audience (aud) claim of incoming JWT tokens.

issuer.description: The expected issuer (iss) claim of incoming JWT tokens.

allOf.$comment: We want the user to provide the JWT property when the provider requires it, and omit JWT when the provider does not require it.

cache.description: Runtime-level cache configuration. Caching is only active when enabled is set to true.

level-2.description: Configuration for the level 2 (distributed) cache and backplane.

enabled.description: Enable or disable the level 2 distributed cache.

provider.description: The provider for the L2 cache. Currently only 'redis' is supported.

connection-string.description: The connection string for the level 2 cache provider.

partition.description: The prefix to use for cache keys in level 2 and backplane, useful in a shared environment to avoid collisions.

level.description: Specifies the response compression level. 'optimal' provides best compression ratio, 'fastest' prioritizes speed, 'none' disables compression.

application-insights.description: Configuration for sending telemetry to Azure Application Insights.

azure-log-analytics.description: Configuration for sending logs to Azure Log Analytics.

auth.description: Authentication credentials for connecting to Azure Log Analytics.

custom-table-name.description: Azure Log Analytics Custom Table Name for entra-id mode

dab-identifier.description: Identifier passed on to Log Analytics

flush-interval-seconds.description: Interval between log batch pushes (in seconds)

custom-table-name.description: Azure Log Analytics Custom Table Name for entra-id mode

file.description: Configuration for writing telemetry logs to a local file.

retained-file-count-limit.description: Maximum number of retained log files.

file-size-limit-bytes.description: Maximum file size in bytes before rolling.

log-level.description: Global configuration of log level, defines logging severity levels for specific classes, when 'null' it will set logging level based on 'host: mode' property

^[a-zA-Z_]\\w{0,}(\\.\\w{1,}){0,}$.description: Only specific namespaces that are used for logging in DAB are able to be used here. The regex pattern checks that it only allows for namespace values. So it only accepts a letter as its first value, from there it accepts any alphanumeric character, each section is divided by '.' and there cannot be more than 1 '.' in a row

health.description: Health check configuration for runtime properties

roles.description: Allowed Roles for Comprehensive Health Endpoint

cache-ttl-seconds.description: Time to live in seconds for the Comprehensive Health Check Report cache entry.

max-query-parallelism.description: The max degree of parallelism for running parallel health check queries.

azure-key-vault.description: Azure Key Vault configuration for storing secrets

retry-policy.description: Retry policy configuration for Azure Key Vault operations

mode.description: Retry mode: fixed or exponential backoff

delay-seconds.description: Initial delay between retries in seconds

max-delay-seconds.description: Maximum delay between retries in seconds (for exponential mode)

network-timeout-seconds.description: Network timeout for requests in seconds

autoentities.description: Defines automatic entity generation rules for MSSQL tables based on include/exclude patterns and defaults.

patterns.description: Pattern matching rules for including/excluding database objects

include.description: MSSQL LIKE pattern for objects to include (e.g., '%.%'). Null includes all.

exclude.description: MSSQL LIKE pattern for objects to exclude (e.g., 'sales.%'). Null excludes none.

name.description: Entity name interpolation pattern using {schema} and {object}. Null defaults to {object}. Must be unique for every entity inside the pattern

oneOf.description: Boolean shorthand: true enables dml-tools only (custom-tool remains false), false disables all MCP functionality.

dml-tools.description: Enable/disable all DML tools with default settings.

ttl-seconds.description: Time-to-live for cached responses in seconds

entities.description: Entities that will be exposed via REST, GraphQL and/or MCP

^.*$.description: Configuration for a single entity exposed via REST, GraphQL, and/or MCP.

description.description: Optional description for the entity. Will be surfaced in generated API documentation and GraphQL schema as comments.

enabled.description: Enable health check endpoint for particular entity

first.description: Number of first 'x' rows to be returned in query.

threshold-ms.description: Health Check maximum time for query to execute (in ms).

source.description: The database object this entity maps to. Can be a table name string or a detailed source object.

oneOf.description: The object in the backend database that is mapped to the entity
The object in the backend database that is mapped to the entity
DEPRECATED. Use the array format instead, which supports per-parameter metadata. Dictionary of parameter names mapped to their default values.
Array of parameter objects with metadata

description.description: Parameter description. Since descriptions for multiple parameters are provided as a comma-separated string, individual parameter descriptions must not contain a comma (',').

key-fields.description: DEPRECATED. Use the 'fields' array and set 'primary-key: true' on each key field instead. List of fields to be used as primary keys.

description.description: Human-readable description of the database object, used for MCP tool discovery and documentation.

fields.description: Defines the fields (columns) exposed for this entity, with metadata.

primary-key.description: Indicates whether this field is a primary key.

rest.description: REST API configuration for this entity. Set to false to disable, true to enable with defaults, or an object for detailed configuration.

path.description: Custom URL path segment for this entity's REST endpoint. Overrides the entity name in the URL.

methods.description: HTTP methods allowed for this entity's REST endpoint. Only relevant for stored-procedure entities.

enabled.description: Allow enabling/disabling REST requests this specific entity.

graphql.description: GraphQL configuration for this entity. Set to false to disable, true to enable with defaults, or an object for detailed configuration.

type.description: Singular and/or plural GraphQL type names for this entity.

operation.description: For stored-procedure entities, specifies whether the GraphQL operation is a query or mutation.

enabled.description: Allow enabling/disabling GraphQL requests for this specific entity.

mappings.description: DEPRECATED. Use the 'fields' array and set the 'alias' property on each field instead. Defines mappings between database column names and their exposed API field names.

relationships.description: Relationship definitions between this entity and other entities.

^.*$.description: Configuration for a single named relationship.

cardinality.description: Defines the cardinality of the relationship between entities.

target.entity.description: The name of the related entity.

source.fields.description: Fields on the source entity used to join to the target entity.

target.fields.description: Fields on the target entity used to join from the source entity.

linking.object.description: The database object used for supporting the M:N relationship

linking.source.fields.description: Fields on the linking object that reference the source entity.

linking.target.fields.description: Fields on the linking object that reference the target entity.

cache.description: Caching configuration for this entity's API responses.

enabled.description: Enable caching of responses for this entity.

oneOf.description: Boolean shorthand: true enables dml-tools only (custom-tool remains false), false disables all MCP functionality.
Detailed MCP configuration for this entity.

dml-tools.description: Enable MCP DML (Data Manipulation Language) tools for this entity. Allows CRUD operations via MCP.

custom-tool.description: Enable MCP custom tool for this entity. Only valid for stored procedures.

mcp.description: Model Context Protocol (MCP) configuration for this entity. Controls whether the entity is exposed via MCP tools.

then.errorMessage: custom-tool can only be enabled for entities with source type 'stored-procedure'.

allOf.$comment: When data-source-files is present (multi-config top-level), only runtime is required. When autoentities is present, data-source is required but entities is not. Otherwise both data-source and entities are required.

singular-plural.description: The GraphQL type name for an entity, specified as either a string (singular name) or an object with explicit singular and plural forms.

singular.description: Defines the singular GraphQL type name for the entity.

plural.description: Defines the plural GraphQL type name for the entity.

action.description: A CRUD action permitted on an entity: create, read, update, delete, or * for all.
